title: leucanthemum paludosum
intro: Leucanthemum paludosum, also known as marsh daisy or marsh marguerite, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum paludosum include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often combined with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its medicinal effects.
